blocks.statement: I can run elastic and perfectly inelastic cart collisions with photogates, compute Σp_before and Σp_after, and report the % difference with one credible source of error.

blocks.whatWeKnow: We've been STATING that Σp is conserved for 7 days. Today we MEASURE it.
blocks.connection: If our lab carts confirm conservation, we can trust the same rule for asteroids. Days 11–13 depend on it.

blocks.title: Investigation 3.1 — Verify conservation
blocks.markdown:
**Driving question:** How well does Σp_before match Σp_after in our lab carts? And does it work for BOTH elastic and perfectly inelastic collisions?

**Equipment:** Vernier dynamics track (1 per pair) + 2 carts · magnetic bumpers (elastic trial) · velcro pads (perfectly inelastic trial) · 2 photogates + LabQuest + Logger Pro or Graphical Analysis · balance to weigh each cart (record m₁ and m₂ first).

**How this lab serves the year's question:** if conservation holds in carts within a few percent, the asteroid math on Days 11–13 stands.

blocks.title: What you do
steps: Level the track. Weigh both carts and record m₁, m₂.
**ELASTIC trials** (magnet bumpers): cart 2 at rest. Push cart 1 gently. Photogates read v₁ᵢ, v₁f, and v₂f.
Compute Σp_before = m₁v₁ᵢ and Σp_after = m₁v₁f + m₂v₂f. Compare.
**PERFECTLY INELASTIC trials** (velcro): cart 2 at rest. Push cart 1; carts stick.
Two trials of each type. Compute % difference for each.

blocks.title: Safety
blocks.markdown: Cart speeds gentle. Photogates clamped firmly. Hands clear of cart paths.

blocks.title: Not in class today?
blocks.markdown: Run the cart-collision simulation below in both bumper modes (magnetic = elastic, velcro = stick). Read the before/after velocities off the sim and fill the data tables from those.

blocks.instruction: Sketch your setup: track, both carts, both photogates. Record m₁ and m₂ on the diagram.
prompts: Label both carts with their measured masses.
Mark where each photogate reads velocity.

blocks.patternPrompt: ELASTIC trials (magnet bumpers, cart 2 at rest). How close are your before/after totals?
PERFECTLY INELASTIC trials (velcro, cart 2 at rest). How close are your before/after totals?
In your trials, did Σp_after match Σp_before? Within what %?
blocks.interpretPrompt: If there's a difference, what physical thing leaked momentum out? Was it the same for elastic and inelastic trials? And the asteroid connection: how does this serve the year's question?
blocks.frame: My elastic Σp matched within ___ %; my perfectly inelastic Σp matched within ___ %. The biggest source of error was ___.

blocks.prompt: Write a one-sentence CLAIM about what your lab showed, then one sentence of EVIDENCE (your numbers), then one sentence of REASONING (why the % difference is small).
